story_arc: Create a cohesive first-person visual diary that feels cinematic, grounded, and alive. Turn the still images into intentional scenes instead of slideshow transitions.
visual_rules: Each shot must start from one source image and resolve into the next image in order.
Keep continuity between shot endings and the next shot beginnings.
Avoid abrupt cuts, abrupt morphs, and slideshow logic.
Prefer grounded motion that can plausibly connect the paired images.
narration_text:
I left the lab when everything was still in its place,
rows of chairs, cold screens, the hum I barely notice anymore.
Outside, the city felt narrow at first, then it opened a little,
and by the time the train arrived, I felt myself settling back into the day.

shots.title: Image1 into Image2
shots.camera: Continuous first-person or observational camera movement that begins in Image1.jpeg and naturally settles into Image2.jpeg, without a restart feeling. Keep enough overlap in framing, movement direction, and visual energy to support a soft handoff to the next adjacent-pair shot.
shots.action: Carry visual momentum from the mood and details of Image1.jpeg into the environment of Image2.jpeg, using plausible movement and continuity anchors rather than abrupt transformation. The shot should feel already in progress at the start and should not come to a dead stop at the end. Avoid powerpoint-style transitions; favor motivated, cinematic motion.
shots.transition_goal: Resolve cleanly from Image1.jpeg to Image2.jpeg while preserving enough camera momentum, framing continuity, and emotional carry-through for the edit to continue without a cut-like reset.
shots.voiceover_line: Move from calm and orderly into gray, overcast, and slightly constrained without breaking continuity.
shots.prompt: Already drifting forward through a quiet computer lab, pass black desks, CDA-marked chairs, and cold desktop monitors in a single observational move; continue toward the exit as reflections and dark frames align, then let the doorway and window glare motivate a smooth reveal into an overcast street with a black metal fence with red panels. Hold the same forward momentum outside, landing on the CHICKEN PLUS sign and two pedestrians walking past the fence, with damp gray light and light street movement carrying the shot onward instead of stopping.
shots.negative_prompt: no slideshow transition, no abrupt cut, no cross-fade, no morphing furniture into street objects, no broken anatomy, no duplicated pedestrians, no text overlays or subtitles, no freeze frame, no hard reset of camera position, no impossible lighting shift

shots.title: Image2 into Image3
shots.camera: Continuous first-person or observational camera movement that begins in Image2.jpeg and naturally settles into Image3.jpeg, without a restart feeling. Keep enough overlap in framing, movement direction, and visual energy to support a soft handoff to the next adjacent-pair shot.
shots.action: Carry visual momentum from the mood and details of Image2.jpeg into the environment of Image3.jpeg, using plausible movement and continuity anchors rather than abrupt transformation. The shot should feel already in progress at the start and should not come to a dead stop at the end. Avoid powerpoint-style transitions; favor motivated, cinematic motion.
shots.transition_goal: Resolve cleanly from Image2.jpeg to Image3.jpeg while preserving enough camera momentum, framing continuity, and emotional carry-through for the edit to continue without a cut-like reset.
shots.voiceover_line: Move from gray, overcast, and slightly constrained into overcast, neutral, everyday urban life without breaking continuity.
shots.prompt: Begin mid-glide beside the black metal fence with red panels under flat gray sky, keeping the CHICKEN PLUS sign and two pedestrians in motion as the camera tracks with them, then eases into a wider sidewalk view through parallax from fence to street. Bare trees, orange no-parking signs, and a brick building with an ALTA sign gradually take over the frame, resolving into a neutral everyday urban block while the pedestrians and ambient traffic continue naturally to preserve forward energy.
shots.negative_prompt: no powerpoint-style transition, no abrupt scene break, no cross-dissolve, no surreal street morphs, no broken limbs or faces, no disappearing fence or sign, no text overlays, no frozen extras, no stop-start camera motion, no unnatural speed ramps

shots.camera: Continuous first-person or observational camera movement that begins in Image3.jpeg and naturally settles into Image4.jpeg, without a restart feeling. Keep enough overlap in framing, movement direction, and visual energy to support a soft handoff to the next adjacent-pair shot.
shots.action: Carry visual momentum from the mood and details of Image3.jpeg into the environment of Image4.jpeg, using plausible movement and continuity anchors rather than abrupt transformation. The shot should feel already in progress at the start and should not come to a dead stop at the end. Avoid powerpoint-style transitions; favor motivated, cinematic motion.
shots.transition_goal: Resolve cleanly from Image3.jpeg to Image4.jpeg while preserving enough camera momentum, framing continuity, and emotional carry-through for the edit to continue without a cut-like reset.
shots.voiceover_line: Move from overcast, neutral, everyday urban life into daily urban commute without breaking continuity.
shots.prompt: Start already moving along the overcast street, with bare trees, orange no-parking signs, and the brick building marked ALTA sliding past in lateral parallax as the camera advances toward transit activity. Use a motivated rack focus and continued forward drift to bring a blue train with a white stripe into dominance, then settle inside the carriage world with STM logo and slogan visible and passengers seated and standing, ending in a lived-in commuter rhythm that still feels in motion.
shots.negative_prompt: no abrupt cut to train interior, no slideshow zoom, no cross-fade, no impossible teleportation, no warped train geometry, no broken passenger anatomy, no duplicated people, no text overlays, no freeze-frame ending, no dead-stop camera reset
